left.label: Without GeoDNS
left.description: Every user worldwide is sent to the same single data center, no matter how far away it is. A user in Chennai and a user in Dubai both make the same long trip across the globe.
traits: Distant users pay a latency tax
No resilience if that region goes down

right.label: With GeoDNS
right.description: DNS itself looks at where the request is coming from and replies with the IP of the nearest data center, before the load balancer is ever involved.
traits: Acts as the first traffic cop, before any load balancer
One region down → reroute to the next nearest
